page: Vultr vcg-b200-248c-2826g-1536vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server configured with an Intel x86_64 architecture. The system features 248 vCPUs with a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 2826.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 11.4 GB of RAM per core.

This server integrates a massive GPU accelerator setup consisting of eight NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs, delivering a combined total of 1536 GB of VRAM. For local storage, the configuration includes 13312 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

The hardware profile represents a high-density resource allocation designed for massive parallel processing. A notable architectural tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation despite the enterprise-grade GPU and memory footprint. Qualitatively, the server offers high resource density for workloads that require substantial VRAM and system memory relative to CPU compute. This configuration is engineered for large-scale GPU-accelerated workloads, including deep learning, large language model training, and complex data science jobs.
description: Vultr vcg-b200-248c-2826g-1536vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server featuring an Intel x86_64 architecture with 248 vCPUs under a shared allocation model. The system is equipped with 2826.0 GB of system memory, yielding 11.4 GB of RAM per core. Its primary accelerator hardware consists of eight NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs providing a total of 1536 GB of VRAM. Local storage is supported by 13312 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. While the shared CPU allocation presents a potential performance tradeoff for CPU-bound tasks, the massive GPU memory and system RAM density make this server highly efficient for large-scale parallel processing. It is designed for intensive workloads such as deep learning, large language model training, and data science jobs.
